title: What Time's Throw In? – Irish Sports TV Schedule Tool
overview: A single-page, self-contained HTML/CSS/JS web tool that helps Irish sports fans quickly find upcoming fixture start times and the TV channel or streaming service broadcasting each event. The tool covers Ireland's five most popular sports—rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing—with a focus on weekend fixtures as the primary viewing window. All schedule data is embedded as static sample fixtures; no server-side runtime, external database, or authentication is required. The page is responsive for desktop and mobile, and includes clear on-page usage instructions so any visitor can immediately understand how to use it.
objectives: Provide a single, easy-to-use page showing upcoming Irish sports fixtures with start times and broadcast channels
Cover the five core Irish-interest sports: rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing
Highlight weekend (Saturday & Sunday) fixtures as the primary viewing window
Deliver a fully self-contained static HTML file with inline CSS and JS, zero external dependencies
Ensure responsive design for both desktop and mobile devices
Include clear on-page usage instructions so first-time visitors can immediately understand the tool

user_stories.i_want: to see a list of upcoming sports fixtures with start times and TV channels on a single page
user_stories.so_that: I can quickly find out when and where to watch my favourite sports without checking multiple sources
acceptance_criteria: Each fixture entry displays: sport type, event/match name, date, start time, and TV channel or streaming service
All times are displayed in Irish Standard Time (IST/GMT)
At least two sample fixtures exist for each of the five core sports (rugby, soccer, GAA, golf, horse racing)
Fixtures are presented in chronological order by default
Sample data includes realistic Irish sporting events, kick-off times, and actual Irish broadcaster names (e.g. RTÉ, TG4, Virgin Media, Sky Sports, Premier Sports, GAAGO, Racing TV)

user_stories.i_want: to filter fixtures by sport type
user_stories.so_that: I can focus on the sports I care about most
acceptance_criteria: Filter controls are visible on the page for each of the five sport categories
Selecting a sport filter shows only fixtures for that sport
An 'All Sports' option is available to clear filters and show every fixture
Filters work entirely client-side with no page reload
The currently active filter is visually indicated

user_stories.i_want: weekend fixtures to be prominently displayed or shown by default
user_stories.so_that: I can immediately see what's on this Saturday and Sunday without extra clicks
acceptance_criteria: Weekend (Saturday and Sunday) fixtures are displayed by default or prominently highlighted on page load
A control allows the user to toggle between weekend-only and all-days views
Weekend fixtures are visually distinguishable from weekday fixtures when both are shown

user_stories.i_want: the page to be responsive and usable on my phone
user_stories.so_that: I can check fixtures on the go
acceptance_criteria: The layout adapts gracefully to screen widths from 320px to 1920px and above
All interactive elements (filters, buttons) have touch-friendly tap targets (minimum 44px)
Text is readable without horizontal scrolling on mobile devices
No content is clipped or inaccessible on small screens

user_stories.i_want: to see clear usage instructions on the page
user_stories.so_that: I understand how to use the tool without any external documentation
acceptance_criteria: Usage instructions are visible on the page on first load without requiring user action
Instructions explain how to filter by sport and how to view weekend fixtures
Instructions are concise (no more than a few sentences) and do not dominate the page layout

user_stories.i_want: the tool to be a single self-contained HTML file with no external dependencies
user_stories.so_that: I can deploy it by simply placing the file at a URL path on any static hosting provider
acceptance_criteria: The entire tool is contained in one .html file
All CSS is in a <style> block within the file (no external stylesheets)
All JavaScript is in a <script> block within the file (no external scripts)
No references to external CDNs, web fonts, images, or APIs
The file opens and functions correctly when loaded directly in the latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

user_stories.i_want: to clearly see which TV channel or streaming service is broadcasting each event
user_stories.so_that: I know exactly where to tune in or which app to open
acceptance_criteria: Each fixture entry clearly displays the broadcast channel or streaming service name
Channel names use recognisable Irish broadcaster branding (e.g. RTÉ 2, TG4, Virgin Media Two, Sky Sports, TNT Sports, Premier Sports, GAAGO, Racing TV)
The channel information is visually prominent and easy to scan within each fixture card or row

user_stories.i_want: to view fixtures grouped or sorted by date
user_stories.so_that: I can plan my viewing for a specific day
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures can be viewed grouped by date with clear date headings
The nearest upcoming date is shown first or highlighted

constraints: Must be a single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JS—no external dependencies of any kind
No external database, authentication, or server-side runtime required
Data is embedded/static—no live API integrations in v1.0
Must function as a static file served at a path URL
Target modern browsers only: latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
Budget capped at $0.50 USD; delivery timeout of 300 seconds
All displayed times in Irish Standard Time (IST/GMT)

assumptions.assumption: The scope of 'top sports' is limited to five categories
assumptions.default: Rugby, soccer, GAA (hurling & football), golf, and horse racing

assumptions.assumption: No user accounts or personalisation features are needed
assumptions.default: All visitors see the same schedule view with client-side filtering only

assumptions.assumption: Weekend is defined as Saturday and Sunday
assumptions.default: Fixtures shown default-filter to Saturday and Sunday

assumptions.assumption: Live data feeds or API integrations are not required for v1.0
assumptions.default: Sport schedule data will be hardcoded as sample/representative fixtures within the HTML file

assumptions.assumption: The tool targets modern browsers only, not legacy
assumptions.default: Latest versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

assumptions.assumption: Time zone for all displayed times is Irish Standard Time
Sample fixture data should represent realistic upcoming Irish sports events
assumptions.default: Use representative fixture names, realistic kick-off times, and actual Irish broadcaster names
